proj-1773719443026.goal: The app has broken imports and exports across files. Go through every file, make sure named vs default exports match their imports, function call arguments match definitions, and only use packages that are in package.json. The project should compile and run cleanly.

_allFileContents.content:
{
  "name": "react-vite-app",
  "private": true,
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "vite",
    "build": "tsc && vite build",
    "preview": "vite preview"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "react": "^18.2.0",
    "react-dom": "^18.2.0",
    "zustand": "^4.4.7",
    "@headlessui/react": "^1.7.17"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/react": "^18.2.15",
    "@types/react-dom": "^18.2.7",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^4.0.3",
    "vite": "^4.4.5",
    "tailwindcss": "^3.3.3",
    "postcss": "^8.4.27",
    "autoprefixer": "^10.4.14",
    "postcss-nested": "^6.0.1"
  }
}

import React from 'react' import StatDisplay from './StatDisplay' import UpgradeShop from './UpgradeShop' function GameUI() { return (
{/* Background elements */}
{[...Array(6)].map((_, i) => (
))}
{/* Main Content */}
{/* Title Section */}

PLUNGER ANNIHILATOR

{/* Gold Stars */}
{[1, 2, 3, 4, 5].map((star) => ( ★ ))}
{/* Main Character Section */}
{/* Water splash effects */}
{/* Character Container */}
{/* Plunger Annihilator Character */}
{/* Glow effect */}
{/* Main character - SVG illustration of monster holding plunger */}
{/* Character Image - SVG placeholder */}
{/* Stats Section */}
{/* Skills Section */}
{/* Start Button */}
{/* Upgrade Shop */}
) } export default GameUI

import React from 'react'
import ReactDOM from 'react-dom/client'
import App from './App'

ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById('root')!).render(
  <React.StrictMode>
    <App />
  </React.StrictMode>,
)

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2020",
    "useDefineForClassFields": true,
    "lib": ["ES2020", "DOM"],
    "module": "ESNext",
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "allowImportingTsExtensions": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "jsx": "react-jsx",
    "strict": true,
    "noUnusedLocals": true,
    "noUnusedParameters": true,
    "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    "preserveSymlinks": true,
    "baseUrl": ".",
    "paths": {
      "@/*": ["./src/*"]
    }
  },
  "include": ["src"],
  "references": [{ "path": "./tsconfig.node.json" }]
}

allTasks.description:
Set up the project structure and configuration files for a React/Vite application with Tailwind CSS. Create package.json with dependencies: react, react-dom, zustand, @headlessui/react, @types/react, @types/react-dom, @vitejs/plugin-react, vite, tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer, postcss-nested. Create index.html with minimal structure including
. Create vite.config.ts with plugin configuration for React and Tailwind. Create tsconfig.json with strict mode enabled, target es2020, lib ['es2020', 'dom'], and include src/**. Create tsconfig.node.json with compilerOptions.target=es2020 and compilerOptions.module=commonjs. Create tailwind.config.js with dark mode, content paths ["./src/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx}"] and theme with custom colors (primary: #ff9900, secondary: #ff6600, accent: #ff3300, background: #1a1a1a, text: #ffffff, card: #2a2a2a), and add custom spacing for 48px, 64px, 80px, 128px. Create postcss.config.js with plugins for tailwindcss, autoprefixer, and postcss-nested. Create src/main.tsx with ReactDOM.createRoot and render . Ensure all files are properly linked and the project can be built with 'npm run dev'.

Implement the core game UI layout and components based on the provided reference image. Design a vertical single-column layout with dark background (gradient from #1a1a1a to #000000). Center the main character (Plunger Annihilator) as the dominant element (40-60% of viewport height) with a dynamic pose holding a plunger, surrounded by water splashes and sewer elements. Above the character, display the title "PLUNGER ANNIHILATOR" in gold gradient text (font-size: 2.5rem, font-weight: 800, text-shadow: 0 0 10px #ff9900). Below the title, show five gold stars (★) in a row. On the right side, display four upgrade icons in a vertical stack (each 60x60px, rounded-lg, border: 1px solid #ff9900, background: #2a2a2a, shadow: 0 0 10px rgba(255,153,0,0.3)). Below the main character, create a stats bar with three sections: HAK (52.310), DEF (31.796), SPD (145) — each in a card (rounded-xl, p-2, bg-opacity-50, backdrop-blur-sm, border: 1px solid #ff9900). Below stats, create three skill buttons (Power Strike, Plunger Smash, Sewer Surge) in a row, each 150x40px, rounded-lg, bg-gradient-to-r from #ff9900 to #ff6600, text-white, font-bold, with icons on left. At the bottom, create a large START button (150x50px, rounded-xl, bg-gradient-to-r from #ff9900 to #ff6600, text-white, font-bold, shadow-lg, hover:shadow-2xl, active:scale-95). All components must use Tailwind classes and be responsive with min-width constraints for key containers in tailwind.config.js.

Update existing components and hooks to implement game logic enhancements. In src/components/StatDisplay.tsx, replace poop.toLocaleString() with formatCompactNumber(poop) imported from utils/formatter.ts. In src/hooks/useGameLogic.ts, modify the auto-generation interval to use setTimeout with a last generation time tracker and delta calculation for precise per-second increments. Update spawnPoopEmojisFromEdges to implement emoji count as Math.min(8, Math.floor(Math.log10(clickPower)) + 1). Fix scroll boundary check in UpgradeShop.tsx by adding null checks for carouselRef.current before accessing scrollWidth. In handleToiletClick, implement the same emoji count calculation. Add a new radial pulse animation for the toilet button by creating a CSS keyframe in src/styles/animations.css: @keyframes radialPulse { 0% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 rgba(255,153,0,0.5); } 100% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 10px rgba(255,153,0,0.1); } } and apply it with animation: radialPulse 0.5s ease-out. In src/components/UpgradeCard.tsx, improve locked state styling with opacity: 0.5 and border: 1px solid #ff3300. Ensure all changes are exported as named exports where applicable.

fixes_needed.issue: Import of '../../utils/formatter' fails during build — the file exists but the import path is incorrect due to missing 'src' in the path or incorrect alias resolution.
fixes_needed.fix_instruction: Update the import to `import { formatCompactNumber } from '../../utils/formatter';` — ensure the path is correct. If using aliases, verify that the alias '@' is properly configured in vite.config.ts and tsconfig.json. Alternatively, use the full path `import { formatCompactNumber } from './utils/formatter';` if the file is in the same directory level as components.

fixes_needed.issue: The @import './animations.css'; statement is placed after @tailwind utilities; which violates CSS syntax — @import must come before any other statements except @charset or @layer.
fixes_needed.fix_instruction: Move the @import './animations.css'; statement to the top of the file, before any @tailwind directives.

fixes_needed.issue: The stylesheet link points to '/src/styles/tailwind.css' which is not a public asset — it should be served from the public directory or built via Vite.
fixes_needed.fix_instruction: Remove the <link> tag and ensure Tailwind CSS is imported via the main entry point (main.tsx) or via a build step. The correct way is to import Tailwind in the main.tsx or via a CSS file that is imported in the entry point.

fixes_needed.issue: The layout does not match the reference image — the main character is a toilet emoji, not a plunger annihilator monster. The design intent is a monster holding a plunger, but the implementation uses emojis.
fixes_needed.fix_instruction: Replace the toilet emoji with a custom monster illustration or SVG. Use a tag or SVG component to display the 'Plunger Annihilator' character as shown in the reference image. Ensure it's centered and takes ~50% of screen height.

fixes_needed.issue: The upgrade shop is fixed at the bottom but does not have a scrollable container — it will overflow on smaller screens. Also, it only shows 4 upgrades, but the requirement is at least 8.
fixes_needed.fix_instruction: Wrap the upgrade cards in a scrollable container with max-height (e.g., max-h-48) and overflow-y-auto. Add at least 4 more upgrades to meet the requirement of 8 total.

fixes_needed.issue: The floating text and poop emojis are created via DOM manipulation, which can cause memory leaks and performance issues. Also, the animation logic is not tied to the game state.
fixes_needed.fix_instruction: Use React state and useEffect to manage floating elements. Consider using a lightweight animation library or React Spring for smoother animations. Clean up timeouts and DOM elements properly.

visual_issues: Main character is a toilet emoji instead of a monster holding a plunger as in the reference image.
Stats panel uses static values instead of dynamic state from Zustand store.
Upgrade shop is not scrollable and shows only 4 upgrades instead of 8+.
Background bubbles are static and do not animate properly — the animation class 'animate-float' is defined but not applied correctly.
The 'START' button is present but not connected to any game logic — it should trigger game initialization.
Floating number animations are not implemented correctly — they are created via DOM manipulation and not rendered by React components.
_reviewResult.summary: The project has a solid foundation with Zustand state management and Tailwind styling, but critical build errors prevent it from running. The visual design significantly deviates from the reference image — the main character is an emoji instead of a monster, and the layout lacks the required scrollable upgrade shop. The build fails due to incorrect CSS import order and a missing import path. Fixing these will allow the game to run, but substantial UI/UX work is needed to match the reference design. The game logic is partially implemented but relies on DOM manipulation which is not ideal for React applications.

global._currentProjectGoal:
Build "Attack on Toilet" from scratch — a vertical mobile-style idle clicker game using React, Tailwind CSS, and Zustand for state management. The layout must be a single centered column like the reference image: main clickable element dominant in the center (50% of screen height), stats overlay at the top, and upgrade shop as a compact scrollable panel at the bottom. No horizontal layouts, no sidebars, no scrollbars on the main page — everything fits in 100vh with overflow-hidden on body.

Core mechanics: Click the toilet emoji (🚽 large, 120px+) to earn poop (💩). Each click spawns poop emojis that fly IN from random screen edges toward the toilet like a vortex, shrinking and fading as they arrive. The number of emojis per click scales with click power: Math.floor(Math.log10(clickPower)) + 1, capped at 8. Floating +number text animates upward from the click point.

Stats panel at top: poop count, click power, per-second rate. Use a compact number formatter (2.8M, 1.2B, 340K) — never toLocaleString for large numbers. The poop count should bounce/pulse when it changes.

Upgrade shop at bottom (max 35vh, scrolls internally): cards color-coded by affordability — gold/amber gradient for buyable, dark red for locked, green with checkmark for owned. Each upgrade has name, description, cost (formatted), and buy button. Include both click multipliers and auto-generators. Start with at least 8 upgrades scaling from 10 to 10,000 cost.

State: Zustand with persist middleware to localStorage. Save poop, clickPower, autoRate, and unlockedUpgrades. Reconstruct non-serializable data on rehydrate. Include a reset game function.

Visual style: dark purple-to-black gradient background, floating semi-transparent bubbles for ambient effect, yellow/gold accent text, glowing pulse on the toilet hover, satisfying squish animation on click (scale 0.9 → 1). Every interaction must have visible feedback.

MUST include: package.json with all deps, index.html, vite.config.js, tailwind.config.js, postcss.config.js. Project must build and run out of the box with npm install && npm run dev. Use image reference as a LAYOUT guide.

avalon-landing.goal: Build a modern landing page for Avalon Platforms™ — a premium cloud infrastructure company. Include: hero section with bold headline "Infrastructure Without Limits", features grid (3 cards: Global Edge Network, Zero-Downtime Deploys, AI-Powered Scaling), pricing section (3 tiers: Starter $29/mo, Pro $99/mo, Enterprise custom), testimonials carousel, and a contact form. Use a dark theme with electric blue (#0066FF) accents. Tech stack: Next.js 14 App Router + Tailwind CSS.

avalon-v2.goal: Recreate this link-in-bio style landing page for Avalon Platforms™. Match the visual design from the reference URL exactly: full-screen video/image background with dark overlay, centered vertical layout, glassmorphism frosted-glass style buttons with semi-transparent backgrounds and subtle borders, the Avalon Platforms™ heading with tagline "Insanely simple marketing technologies", icon row, feature link buttons (Create stunning micro sites, Make incredible ads with AI, Fast & simple email marketing, Go to Avalon Platforms), Clone this site button, and email input with social icons at the bottom. Use the Geist font family. Dark/moody aesthetic with glass-effect UI elements. Tech stack: Next.js 14 App Router + Tailwind CSS.

idle-clicker.goal: Create an "idle clicker game" similar to cookie clicker. You can choose the design and theme. Be as creative as you like. Use the reference photo as inspiration.

_finalReview.summary: The project has solid foundation with correct layout, state management, and basic mechanics implemented. However, several key features need completion including the floating number animation system and full upgrade functionality.

suggestions.preview: Fix FloatingNumber component and integrate poop counter pulse animation
suggestions.detail: In src/components/FloatingNumber.tsx, fix the missing closing tags and unclosed div. The component should properly render +number text with upward animation. In StatDisplay.tsx, add bounce-pulse class to the value display when it changes by tracking previous value in state. Add useEffect hook that compares current and previous poop count from store, triggering animation when they differ. Update animations.css with proper keyframes for this pulse effect. Also ensure floating numbers are spawned correctly on click by properly integrating FloatingNumber component in ToiletButton.tsx.

suggestions.preview: Convert DOM element creation to React components for emoji animations
suggestions.detail: In useGameLogic.ts, replace direct DOM manipulation with proper React components. Create a new PoopEmoji component that handles its own animation using CSS transitions or Framer Motion. Similarly create FloatingText component. Move the poopEmojisRef and floatingTextRef state into the game store for better reactivity. Update the spawn functions to use these React components instead of creating DOM elements directly. This will improve performance and maintainability while ensuring proper React rendering lifecycle.

suggestions.preview: Implement full upgrade system with game store integration
suggestions.detail: In src/store.ts, expand the state to include upgrades array with cost, multiplier, and level properties for each. Add functions to purchase upgrades that check affordability and update clickPower/autoRate accordingly. In UpgradeShop.tsx, replace mock data with dynamic list from store. Each upgrade should have proper cost display using formatCompactNumber, and buy button should trigger purchase function. When purchased, change card appearance to owned state (green with checkmark). Add visual feedback for successful purchases through toast notifications or temporary glow effect on the upgraded card.

parameters.jsCode:
const aggregate = $('Aggregate All Results').first().json;
return [{ json: aggregate }];
return [{ json: $('P3: Review Parse').first().json }];
return [{ json: $('P4: Fix Write').first().json }];
